dataset.notes: Merges 2 raw entries. Two mutually blind model annotators (Fable 5 + Opus 4.8), exact kappa 0.677 / level-2 0.806; adjudicator-shares-model-with-A bias recorded (60/64 to A). prov.summary: Zero human annotation anywhere: Annotator A = Fable 5, Annotator B = Opus 4.8 (both Claude-family LLMs), adjudicator = Fable 5 — the same model as annotator A — which resolved 60/64 disagreements to its own labels, a self-preference bias the crosswalk doc itself records. Two contamination leads force the worse label over LLM-ASSISTED: (1) both annotators saw the bridge's top-6 candidates and kept one for ~45-58% of rows (A 89/200, B 116/200), so about half the gold labels ARE the output of the very bridge system the gold is then used to score (top-1 17.5%, gold-in-top-6 45%) — circular anchoring that inflates bridge-vs-gold metrics; (2) same-model-family adjudication of its own family's disputed output, plus κ measured between two same-family models. The documented human gate (MG ~50-row spot-check) is explicitly "not started" and "optional", so no human has ever reviewed any row — even LLM-ASSISTED's gate condition is only prospective. Documentation quality is exemplary (the biases are self-declared in the artifact and the A58 paper states the limitation), but FEATURES_INDEX C19 and the kosha manifest register it unqualified as "adjudicated gold (kappa 0.677)".

prov.llm_involvement: Annotator A AND adjudicator: Fable 5 (claude-fable-5); Annotator B: Opus 4.8 (claude-opus-4-8) — both Claude-family, mutually blind under one written protocol, free-form write-ins allowed. prov.kappa_note: kappa 0.677 (exact leaf) / 0.806 (level-2 prefix) covers the WHOLE 200-row gold set (n=200, not a subsample), computed by data/semdom_ak_metrics.py over the two full annotator files. But it is inter-LLM agreement between two same-family (Claude) models — not human-human or human-model IRR — so correlated model priors plausibly inflate it, and it says nothing about correctness against human judgment. The 99.2%-style framing elsewhere in the org does not apply here; no human agreement number exists for this dataset.

prov.summary: The "two-rater gold annotation" contains zero human annotators: rater A is the eval pipeline's own mechanical scorer (eval-result.json verdicts) and rater B is Claude Fable 5 (claude-fable-5), an LLM — sanctioned by a documented author decision of 11-07-2026 written into GOLD_PROTOCOL.md, with AI-role disclosure and a human-adjudication gate for disagreements. That human gate is documented and tooled (blind extraction sheets, an interactive adjudication HTML, pre-drafted verdict variants) but has not actually been exercised: both flagged disagreements (vedic-classical-anachronism-r02 and dict-zone-order-r05) still await a human decision. Torn between SILVER and LLM-ASSISTED, LLM-ASSISTED is the worse and the correct one — no human ever rated a case or comprehensively reviewed the LLM's judgments; the human touchpoints are a protocol decision and two pending adjudications. The published numbers are not CONTAMINATED because every benchmarked run is DeepSeek (v4-flash/v4-pro) while the case authors and rater B are Claude-family, but two contamination leads stand: for H1213 the same Fable 5 session authored the dict gold cases, ran the benchmark, AND served as rater B (blindness only at sheet level), and GOLD_PROTOCOL.md explicitly plans `--provider anthropic` suites against these Claude-co-authored cases, which would be same-family gold. prov.summary: Every annotation stage is LLM-only within one model family: annotator A = Sonnet 5 (claude-sonnet-5) mechanical checks + Fable 5 (claude-fable-5) adjudication; annotator B = Opus 4.8 (claude-opus-4-8) x10 blind agents; the cross-family annotator C was built but never run, and the repo's own HUMAN_ANCHOR_NEEDED.md concedes there is no human anchor. Human involvement is real but partial — MG audited/downgraded 5 SHS rows and gated the CORRECTIONS filing — which would support LLM-ASSISTED, but two documented contamination paths outrank it. First, detectors/gold_corrections.tsv, used by detectors/eval.py as "held-out" gold for recall/F0.5/harm of the detector suite, is built entirely from that same suite's tier-A candidate output (combined_candidates.txt), so suite-invisible typos are structurally absent — gold built from the evaluated system's own output. Second, the corrections were generated by Anthropic models (Sonnet/Opus triage) and then "verified" and IRR-annotated exclusively by Anthropic models — same-family judging its own family's output, the exact self-preference confound the repo cites (D9) and never closed. Torn between LLM-ASSISTED and CONTAMINATED, the worse label applies per the rules.
